title: montia parvifolia var. flagellaris
intro: Montia parvifolia var. flagellaris, also known as creeping montia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Portulacaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Montia parvifolia var. flagellaris can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for herbal use, although its safety and efficacy need further research and validation.
